rawText:
Two American astronauts awaiting their return flight from the International Space Station (ISS) have stayed busy carrying out a series of scientific experiments.

The U.S. space agency NASA has said the pair -- Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams -- will remain at the ISS until late March or early April. When they arrived at the orbiting laboratory in June 2024, the astronauts expected to stay in space for just one week.

Their trip was a test flight of Boeing's Starliner spacecraft. While Starliner safely transported the two to the ISS, the spacecraft had several problems that persuaded NASA to send it back to Earth for further testing without the astronauts onboard.

NASA's current plan calls for Wilmore and Williams to fly back on a SpaceX spacecraft in late March at the earliest. NASA has been using SpaceX's Crew Dragon to regularly carry astronauts and supplies to the ISS since 2020.

The astronauts have been using their extended mission to carry out experiments. In addition, Williams stepped out of the space station earlier this month to complete a spacewalk.

Another NASA astronaut, Nick Hague, joined Williams on the spacewalk on January 16. The two repaired a piece of equipment that helps guide the movements of the ISS. The spacewalk also included work on an X-ray telescope, as well as maintenance activities for other ISS systems.

NASA said Wilmore and Williams have tested different methods for growing crops in space. Such methods could support humans who have longer stays in space in the future.

One experiment is called the Plant Water Management investigation. NASA said this experiment uses the physical properties of fluids to overcome the lack of gravity and provide hydration to plants.

Another experiment examines a new way to grow vegetables, plants and flowers inside the ISS to provide nutrition and beauty. As part of this experiment, Wilmore installed a new light meter inside the ISS.

NASA also said that throughout the current mission, Williams had deployed and observed three free-flying robots called Astrobee. This robotic experiment seeks to examine how such robots could assist astronauts with daily duties and maintenance work.

The agency said the square-shaped robots are designed to help scientists and engineers develop and test new robotic technologies for use in microgravity environments.

NASA astronauts visiting the ISS have a long history of capturing images of Earth from space. And this is another activity Wilmore and Williams have carried out. The ISS gives astronauts a "window to the world" as they orbit 400 kilometers above their home planet.

In early August, Williams used ham radio technology to connect with students from Banda Aceh, Indonesia. NASA explained that students get the chance to communicate with the astronauts when the ISS passes overhead.

overview.title: Astronauts Stay Busy on International Space Station Awaiting Flight Home

sentences.original: Two American astronauts awaiting their return flight from the International Space Station (ISS) have stayed busy carrying out a series of scientific experiments.
sentences.translation: 국제우주정거장(ISS)에서 귀환 비행을 기다리는 두 미국 우주비행사가 일련의 과학 실험을 수행하며 바쁘게 지내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: awaiting their return flight from the International Space Station
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (수식)

grammarElements.text: carrying out a series of scientific experiments
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 행위)

vocabulary.example: The astronaut spent six months on the ISS.
We are awaiting further instructions.
Scientists carried out the experiment carefully.

expressions.usage: 쉬지 않고 활동하고 있음을 나타낼 때
여러 개의 연속적인 사건이나 활동을 소개할 때

patterns.explanation: 기다리는 동안 활발하게 활동하고 있음을 전달하는 뉴스 리드 패턴
examples: Workers awaiting assignments have stayed busy training.
Students awaiting results have stayed busy studying.

drillQuestions.question: The astronauts have stayed busy _____ out experiments.
They are _____ their return flight.

sentences.notes: 뉴스 기사의 리드(lead) 문장으로, 누가(우주비행사), 무엇을(실험 수행), 어디서(ISS)를 한 문장에 담았다. awaiting은 waiting for보다 격식적인 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: carrying out a series of scientific experiments

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역피라미드(inverted pyramid) — 가장 중요한 정보를 첫 문장에 배치하는 뉴스 구조

sentences.original: The U.S. space agency NASA has said the pair -- Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams -- will remain at the ISS until late March or early April.
sentences.translation: 미국 항공우주국 NASA는 두 사람 -- 부치 윌모어와 수니 윌리엄스 -- 이 3월 말이나 4월 초까지 ISS에 남아 있을 것이라고 말했다.

grammarElements.text: the pair -- Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams --
grammarElements.label: 간접 인용 주어 (동격 삽입)

grammarElements.text: will remain at the ISS until late March or early April
grammarElements.label: 간접 인용 내용

vocabulary.example: The temperature will remain high this week.
The pair worked well together.

expressions.usage: 공식 기관의 발표를 간접 인용할 때 현재완료로 현재성을 유지

patterns.explanation: 공식 기관의 발표를 간접 인용하는 뉴스 보도 패턴
examples: The government has said the policy will remain until December.
The company has said the project will continue until next year.

drillQuestions.question: NASA has _____ the mission will continue.
They will _____ at the station until April.

sentences.notes: 대시(dash)로 삽입된 "Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams"는 동격(apposition)으로, "the pair"가 누구인지를 구체적으로 밝힌다. 뉴스에서 자주 사용되는 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: the pair -- Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams --
koreanAlignment.ko: 두 사람 -- 부치 윌모어와 수니 윌리엄스 -- 이

koreanAlignment.en: until late March or early April

sentences.original: When they arrived at the orbiting laboratory in June 2024, the astronauts expected to stay in space for just one week.
sentences.translation: 2024년 6월에 궤도 실험실에 도착했을 때, 우주비행사들은 우주에서 단 1주일만 머물 것으로 예상했다.

grammarElements.text: When they arrived at the orbiting laboratory in June 2024
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: to stay in space for just one week
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: The orbiting satellite sends data back to Earth.
The laboratory is equipped with modern technology.
We expected the results to be positive.

expressions.usage: just를 사용하여 짧은 기간을 강조할 때

patterns.explanation: 과거의 예상과 실제 상황 사이의 차이를 암시하는 패턴
examples: When she started the project, she expected to finish it in a month.
When they set out, they expected to return within a day.

drillQuestions.question: They expected to _____ for only one week.
_____ they arrived, the plan was different.

sentences.notes: "just one week"와 실제 9개월 이상의 체류 사이의 극적인 차이가 이 기사의 핵심 뉴스 가치이다. orbiting laboratory는 ISS의 바꿔 부르기(paraphrase)로, 같은 단어의 반복을 피하는 저널리즘 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: to stay in space for just one week

sentences.original: Their trip was a test flight of Boeing's Starliner spacecraft.
sentences.translation: 그들의 여행은 보잉의 스타라이너 우주선의 시험 비행이었다.

grammarElements.text: a test flight of Boeing's Starliner spacecraft
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: The test flight was scheduled for next week.
The spacecraft docked with the space station.

expressions.usage: 새로운 항공우주 기체의 시험 운항을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 목적이나 성격을 간결하게 규정하는 패턴
examples: Their visit was a demonstration of the new technology.
Their project was a test case of the new policy.

drillQuestions.question: Their trip _____ a test flight.
It was a test flight _____ Boeing's spacecraft.

sentences.notes: Boeing Starliner는 SpaceX Crew Dragon과 경쟁하는 유인 우주선이다. 기사의 배경 정보를 간결하게 제공하는 문장이다.

sentences.original: While Starliner safely transported the two to the ISS, the spacecraft had several problems that persuaded NASA to send it back to Earth for further testing without the astronauts onboard.
sentences.translation: 스타라이너가 두 사람을 ISS로 안전하게 수송했지만, 우주선에 여러 문제가 있어서 NASA가 우주비행사 없이 우주선을 지구로 돌려보내 추가 시험을 하도록 설득했다.

grammarElements.text: While Starliner safely transported the two to the ISS
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절

grammarElements.text: that persuaded NASA to send it back to Earth for further testing without the astronauts onboard
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: The ship transports goods across the ocean.
She persuaded him to change his mind.
All passengers were safely onboard.

expressions.usage: 성공적인 부분과 문제를 동시에 보도할 때
더 많은 검사나 시험이 필요함을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: While [subject] [past verb] safely, [subject] had [problems] that [verb]ed [someone] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 측면(안전 수송)과 부정적 측면(문제 발생)을 대조하는 뉴스 패턴
examples: While the surgery went safely, the recovery had complications that required further treatment.
While the launch succeeded, the mission had setbacks that forced a redesign.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the flight went safely, there were problems.
The problems _____ NASA to change plans.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 기사의 핵심 배경을 설명한다. While은 양보의 의미로 사용되어 "~했지만"을 나타낸다. persuade는 의인화(personification)로, 실제로 "문제들"이 NASA를 설득한 것이 아니라 문제로 인해 NASA가 결정을 내린 것이다.

koreanAlignment.en: While Starliner safely transported the two to the ISS
koreanAlignment.ko: 스타라이너가 두 사람을 ISS로 안전하게 수송했지만

koreanAlignment.en: that persuaded NASA to send it back to Earth

sentences.original: NASA's current plan calls for Wilmore and Williams to fly back on a SpaceX spacecraft in late March at the earliest.
sentences.translation: NASA의 현재 계획은 윌모어와 윌리엄스가 빨라야 3월 말에 SpaceX 우주선으로 귀환하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: Wilmore and Williams to fly back on a SpaceX spacecraft
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + to부정사

grammarElements.text: in late March at the earliest
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

vocabulary.example: The plan calls for major changes.
The project will be completed in June at the earliest.

expressions.usage: 공식 계획을 보도할 때 자주 사용
특정 항공기나 우주선으로 귀환함을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: [Organization]'s plan calls for [someone] to [verb] in [time] at the earliest
patterns.explanation: 기관의 공식 계획과 최선의 일정을 보도하는 뉴스 패턴
examples: The company's plan calls for the product to launch in September at the earliest.
The government's plan calls for construction to begin next year at the earliest.

drillQuestions.question: The plan calls _____ a new approach.
The launch will happen in June at the _____.

sentences.notes: "calls for"는 "요구하다"가 아니라 "~을 예정/계획하다"의 의미로 사용되었다. "at the earliest"는 최소 시기를 나타내며, 더 지연될 수 있음을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Wilmore and Williams to fly back
in late March at the earliest

sentences.original: NASA has been using SpaceX's Crew Dragon to regularly carry astronauts and supplies to the ISS since 2020.
sentences.translation: NASA는 2020년부터 SpaceX의 크루 드래곤을 사용하여 우주비행사와 보급품을 ISS로 정기적으로 수송해 왔다.

grammarElements.text: to regularly carry astronauts and supplies to the ISS
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적)

vocabulary.example: Medical supplies were sent to the disaster area.
She exercises regularly.

expressions.usage: 과거 특정 시점부터 현재까지 계속되는 활동을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 특정 도구나 수단을 지속적으로 사용해온 기간을 설명하는 패턴
examples: The school has been using tablets to teach students since 2019.
The hospital has been using AI to diagnose patients since 2021.

drillQuestions.question: NASA has been _____ SpaceX rockets since 2020.
They have been working together _____ 2018.

sentences.notes: 현재완료진행형(has been using)은 2020년부터 현재까지 지속적인 사용을 강조한다. Crew Dragon은 SpaceX가 NASA를 위해 개발한 유인 우주선이다.

koreanAlignment.en: to regularly carry astronauts and supplies

sentences.original: The astronauts have been using their extended mission to carry out experiments.
sentences.translation: 우주비행사들은 연장된 임무 기간을 활용하여 실험을 수행해 왔다.

vocabulary.example: They had an extended stay due to the delay.
The space mission lasted six months.

patterns.explanation: 주어진 기회나 시간을 활용하여 무언가를 해왔음을 표현하는 패턴
examples: Students have been using the break to prepare for exams.
She has been using her free time to learn a new language.

drillQuestions.question: They have been _____ their time wisely.
The extended mission was used to carry _____ experiments.

sentences.notes: 1주일 예정이었던 임무가 9개월 이상으로 연장된 상황에서, 시간을 낭비하지 않고 실험에 활용하고 있다는 긍정적 프레이밍이 인상적이다.

sentences.original: In addition, Williams stepped out of the space station earlier this month to complete a spacewalk.
sentences.translation: 또한, 윌리엄스는 이번 달 초 우주유영을 완료하기 위해 우주정거장 밖으로 나갔다.

vocabulary.example: She stepped out of the car.
The astronaut performed a spacewalk to repair the satellite.

patterns.explanation: 추가 정보를 이어서 전달하는 뉴스 구조
examples: In addition, the team visited the factory to inspect the equipment.
In addition, researchers published a paper to share their findings.

drillQuestions.question: _____ addition, she completed the training course.
Williams stepped _____ of the station for a spacewalk.

sentences.notes: "stepped out of the space station"은 우주유영(EVA: Extra-Vehicular Activity)을 일상적인 표현으로 묘사한 것이다. 마치 방에서 나가듯 표현했지만 실제로는 우주 진공으로 나가는 극도로 위험한 활동이다.

sentences.original: Another NASA astronaut, Nick Hague, joined Williams on the spacewalk on January 16.
sentences.translation: 또 다른 NASA 우주비행사인 닉 헤이그가 1월 16일 우주유영에서 윌리엄스와 합류했다.

vocabulary.example: She joined the team last month.

patterns.explanation: 추가 참여자를 소개하는 뉴스 패턴. 동격 삽입으로 이름을 명시한다.
examples: Another engineer, Sarah Kim, joined the project on Monday.
Another doctor, Dr. Lee, joined the surgery on March 5.

drillQuestions.question: Nick Hague _____ Williams on the spacewalk.
Another astronaut joined her _____ the mission.

sentences.notes: 동격 삽입(apposition)으로 "Nick Hague"를 "Another NASA astronaut"와 동격으로 배치하여 소개한다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 또 다른 NASA 우주비행사인 닉 헤이그가

sentences.original: The two repaired a piece of equipment that helps guide the movements of the ISS.
sentences.translation: 두 사람은 ISS의 움직임을 안내하는 데 도움을 주는 장비를 수리했다.

grammarElements.text: that helps guide the movements of the ISS
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: The technician repaired the broken machine.
The lab has new scientific equipment.
The system guides the spacecraft through orbit.

expressions.usage: 불가산 명사인 equipment을 셀 때 사용하는 단위

patterns.explanation: 수리한 장비의 기능을 관계절로 설명하는 패턴
examples: They repaired a sensor that helps monitor temperature.
She repaired a device that helps track location.

drillQuestions.question: They repaired a _____ of equipment.
The system helps _____ the movements of the station.

sentences.notes: equipment은 불가산 명사이므로 "a piece of equipment"으로 세어야 한다. "equipments"는 잘못된 표현이다.

sentences.original: NASA said Wilmore and Williams have tested different methods for growing crops in space.
sentences.translation: NASA는 윌모어와 윌리엄스가 우주에서 작물을 재배하기 위한 다양한 방법을 시험했다고 밝혔다.

grammarElements.text: Wilmore and Williams have tested different methods
grammarElements.label: 간접 인용절

vocabulary.example: Scientists developed new methods for testing.
Farmers grow various crops in the region.

expressions.usage: 여러 접근 방식을 시도하고 있음을 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 기관이 발표한 연구 활동을 간접 인용하는 뉴스 패턴
examples: The university said researchers have tested new treatments for curing the disease.
The agency said engineers have tested different designs for building the bridge.

drillQuestions.question: They have tested different _____ for growing food.
NASA said they tested methods _____ growing crops.

sentences.notes: 우주에서의 식량 재배는 미래 화성 탐사를 위한 핵심 기술이다. "have tested"는 현재완료로, 시험이 최근까지 계속되었음을 나타낸다.

sentences.original: Such methods could support humans who have longer stays in space in the future.
sentences.translation: 그러한 방법은 미래에 더 긴 우주 체류를 하는 인간을 지원할 수 있을 것이다.

grammarElements.text: humans who have longer stays in space
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The program supports students with scholarships.
Her stay in the hospital lasted a week.

expressions.usage: 앞서 언급한 방법을 다시 가리킬 때

patterns.explanation: 연구의 실용적 의의를 설명하는 패턴. could로 가능성을 나타낸다.
examples: Such technology could support workers who operate in extreme conditions.
Such programs could support families who face financial difficulties.

drillQuestions.question: Such methods _____ support future space missions.
Humans who have longer _____ in space need food.

sentences.notes: could는 "~할 수 있을 것이다"라는 미래의 가능성을 나타낸다. 이 문장은 현재 실험이 왜 중요한지 그 의의를 설명하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: humans who have longer stays in space
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 긴 우주 체류를 하는 인간을

sentences.original: One experiment is called the Plant Water Management investigation.
sentences.translation: 한 실험은 식물 수분 관리 연구라고 불린다.

vocabulary.example: The investigation revealed important findings.
Water management is critical in dry areas.

patterns.explanation: 여러 항목 중 하나를 공식 명칭과 함께 소개하는 패턴
examples: One project is called the Green Energy Initiative.
One program is called the Youth Leadership Academy.

drillQuestions.question: The experiment is _____ the Plant Water Management investigation.
One experiment _____ called the hydration study.

sentences.notes: "is called"은 명칭을 소개하는 수동태 구조이다. "Plant Water Management"는 고유명사처럼 대문자로 표기되어 공식 실험명임을 나타낸다.

sentences.original: NASA said this experiment uses the physical properties of fluids to overcome the lack of gravity and provide hydration to plants.
sentences.translation: NASA는 이 실험이 유체의 물리적 특성을 이용하여 중력 부족을 극복하고 식물에 수분을 공급한다고 말했다.

grammarElements.text: this experiment uses the physical properties of fluids
grammarElements.label: 간접 인용절

grammarElements.text: to overcome the lack of gravity and provide hydration to plants
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적)

vocabulary.example: Fluids behave differently in space.
She overcame many challenges to succeed.
Proper hydration is essential for health.
There is very little gravity on the Moon.

patterns.explanation: 실험이 특정 원리를 활용하여 문제를 해결하고 목표를 달성하는 과정을 설명하는 패턴
examples: The system uses AI to overcome language barriers and translate in real-time.
The device uses solar energy to overcome power shortages and run continuously.

drillQuestions.question: The experiment uses fluid properties to _____ gravity issues.
They provide _____ to the plants in space.

sentences.notes: 무중력 환경에서는 물이 아래로 흐르지 않기 때문에 식물에 물을 공급하는 것이 어렵다. 이 실험은 유체의 표면장력 등 물리적 성질을 이용하여 이 문제를 해결하려는 것이다.

koreanAlignment.en: to overcome the lack of gravity

sentences.original: Another experiment examines a new way to grow vegetables, plants and flowers inside the ISS to provide nutrition and beauty.
sentences.translation: 또 다른 실험은 ISS 내부에서 채소, 식물, 꽃을 재배하여 영양과 아름다움을 제공하는 새로운 방법을 검토한다.

grammarElements.text: a new way to grow vegetables, plants and flowers inside the ISS
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The doctor examined the patient carefully.
Good nutrition is essential for astronauts.

patterns.pattern: Another [noun] examines a new way to [verb] ... to provide [benefit]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 방법과 그 이점을 설명하는 연구 보도 패턴
examples: Another study examines a new way to teach online to provide better engagement.
Another project examines a new way to recycle plastic to provide cleaner oceans.

drillQuestions.question: Another experiment _____ a new growing method.
The plants provide nutrition _____ beauty.

sentences.notes: "nutrition and beauty"라는 조합이 인상적이다. 우주에서 식물은 단순히 식량뿐만 아니라 시각적 아름다움과 정서적 안정감도 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a new way to grow vegetables, plants and flowers
koreanAlignment.ko: 채소, 식물, 꽃을 재배하는 새로운 방법을

sentences.original: NASA also said that throughout the current mission, Williams had deployed and observed three free-flying robots called Astrobee.
sentences.translation: NASA는 또한 현재 임무 기간 동안 윌리엄스가 아스트로비라고 불리는 세 대의 자유 비행 로봇을 배치하고 관찰했다고 밝혔다.

vocabulary.example: The army deployed troops to the border.
Scientists observed the behavior of the animals.
The free-flying drone navigated autonomously.

expressions.usage: 임무 전 기간에 걸쳐 수행된 활동을 설명할 때
고유명사를 소개할 때 사용하는 수동태 축약

patterns.pattern: [Organization] said that [subject] had [past participle] and [past participle] [objects]
patterns.explanation: 과거완료 병렬 구조로 보고된 활동을 나열하는 뉴스 패턴
examples: The report said the team had designed and tested the prototype.
NASA said the crew had collected and analyzed samples.

drillQuestions.question: Williams had _____ three robots during the mission.
The robots are _____ Astrobee.

sentences.notes: Astrobee는 NASA가 개발한 정육면체 모양의 자유 비행 로봇으로, ISS 내부에서 자율적으로 날아다닌다. deploy는 군사 용어에서 유래했지만 기술 분야에서도 자주 사용된다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 아스트로비라고 불리는 세 대의 자유 비행 로봇을

sentences.original: This robotic experiment seeks to examine how such robots could assist astronauts with daily duties and maintenance work.
sentences.translation: 이 로봇 실험은 그러한 로봇들이 우주비행사의 일상 업무와 유지보수 작업을 어떻게 도울 수 있는지 조사하고자 한다.

grammarElements.text: how such robots could assist astronauts
grammarElements.label: 간접 의문문 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: with daily duties and maintenance work
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (내용)

vocabulary.example: The study seeks to understand the causes.
The robot assists with daily tasks.
Regular maintenance keeps the equipment running.

expressions.usage: 매일 수행해야 하는 임무를 설명할 때

patterns.explanation: 실험의 목적을 간접 의문문으로 설명하는 학술/뉴스 패턴
examples: The study seeks to examine how AI could improve healthcare.
The project seeks to examine how renewable energy could replace fossil fuels.

drillQuestions.question: The experiment _____ to examine robot capabilities.
Robots could _____ astronauts with maintenance.

sentences.notes: "seeks to"는 "tries to"보다 격식적인 표현이다. 간접 의문문 "how such robots could assist"에서 could는 가능성을 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: This robotic experiment seeks to examine
how such robots could assist astronauts
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러한 로봇들이 우주비행사를 어떻게 도울 수 있는지

koreanAlignment.en: with daily duties and maintenance work

sentences.original: The agency said the square-shaped robots are designed to help scientists and engineers develop and test new robotic technologies for use in microgravity environments.
sentences.translation: NASA는 정사각형 모양의 로봇이 과학자와 엔지니어가 미세중력 환경에서 사용할 새로운 로봇 기술을 개발하고 시험하는 것을 돕기 위해 설계되었다고 밝혔다.

grammarElements.text: to help scientists and engineers develop and test new robotic technologies
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적)

vocabulary.example: The square-shaped building stands out.
Experiments in microgravity produce unique results.
The engineer designed a new system.

patterns.pattern: [Something] is designed to help [people] [verb] and [verb] [things] for use in [environment]
patterns.explanation: 제품의 설계 목적과 사용 환경을 설명하는 기술 보도 패턴
examples: The software is designed to help teachers create and share lesson plans for use in classrooms.
The tool is designed to help doctors diagnose and treat diseases for use in rural areas.

drillQuestions.question: The robots are _____ to help scientists.
These technologies are for use in _____ environments.

sentences.notes: "the agency"는 NASA를 다른 표현으로 바꿔 쓴 것(paraphrase)이다. microgravity는 "micro(매우 작은) + gravity(중력)"의 합성어로, ISS에서의 거의 무중력 상태를 의미한다.

koreanAlignment.en: develop and test new robotic technologies
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 로봇 기술을 개발하고 시험하는 것을 돕기 위해

sentences.original: NASA astronauts visiting the ISS have a long history of capturing images of Earth from space.
sentences.translation: ISS를 방문하는 NASA 우주비행사들은 우주에서 지구 사진을 찍어온 오랜 역사를 가지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: a long history of capturing images of Earth from space
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: She captured a beautiful sunset on camera.
The school has a long history of excellence.

patterns.explanation: 특정 그룹의 오래된 전통을 소개하는 패턴
examples: Scientists exploring the ocean have a long history of discovering new species.
Artists visiting Paris have a long history of finding inspiration.

drillQuestions.question: Astronauts have a long _____ of taking photos from space.
They have been _____ images for decades.

sentences.notes: "have a long history of"는 관행의 전통성을 강조한다. 우주에서의 지구 사진 촬영은 1960년대부터 시작된 전통으로, 가장 유명한 것은 1968년 아폴로 8호의 "지구돋이(Earthrise)" 사진이다.

koreanAlignment.en: of capturing images of Earth from space

sentences.original: The ISS gives astronauts a "window to the world" as they orbit 400 kilometers above their home planet.
sentences.translation: ISS는 우주비행사들이 고향 행성 위 400킬로미터 상공을 궤도 비행할 때 "세계를 향한 창"을 제공한다.

grammarElements.text: as they orbit 400 kilometers above their home planet
grammarElements.label: 시간/상황 부사절

vocabulary.example: The satellite orbits the Earth every 90 minutes.
Astronauts always miss their home planet.

expressions.usage: 외부 세계를 관찰할 수 있는 기회나 시각을 비유적으로 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 장소가 사람에게 제공하는 독특한 경험을 은유로 표현하는 패턴
examples: The mountaintop gives hikers a "gateway to the sky" as they climb higher.
The library gives readers a "portal to other worlds" as they explore.

drillQuestions.question: The ISS _____ astronauts a unique view of Earth.
They orbit 400 kilometers _____ the Earth.

sentences.notes: "window to the world"는 따옴표로 묶여 있어 비유적 표현임을 나타낸다. "home planet"이라는 표현은 우주의 관점에서 지구를 바라보는 독특한 시각을 제공한다.

sentences.original: In early August, Williams used ham radio technology to connect with students from Banda Aceh, Indonesia.
sentences.translation: 8월 초, 윌리엄스는 아마추어 라디오 기술을 사용하여 인도네시아 반다아체의 학생들과 교신했다.

grammarElements.text: to connect with students from Banda Aceh, Indonesia
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적)

vocabulary.example: Ham radio operators can communicate across the globe.
The teacher connected with students online.

patterns.explanation: 기술을 사용하여 특정 지역의 사람들과 소통한 것을 설명하는 패턴
examples: The teacher used video conferencing to connect with students from rural areas.
The organization used social media to connect with volunteers from around the world.

drillQuestions.question: Williams _____ ham radio to talk to students.
She connected _____ students from Indonesia.

sentences.notes: ham radio(아마추어 무선)는 ISS가 지구 상공을 통과할 때 짧은 시간 동안 가능한 교신 방법이다. ARISS(Amateur Radio on the ISS) 프로그램의 일환으로, 전 세계 학생들이 우주비행사와 대화할 수 있는 교육적 기회이다.
